Run started for task: Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S1-ahc001-atcoder-ad
Title: S1. AHC001 / AtCoder Ad

Problem:
Place many non-overlapping axis-aligned ad rectangles in a 10000 x 10000 square so each advertiser gets a rectangle containing its requested point and with area close to its requested target area.

Grading target:
Continuous score from advertiser satisfaction.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S1-ahc001-atcoder-ad
Title: S1. AHC001 / AtCoder Ad

Problem:
Place many non-overlapping axis-aligned ad rectangles in a 10000 x 10000 square so each advertiser gets a rectangle containing its requested point and with area close to its requested target area.

Grading target:
Continuous score from advertiser satisfaction.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: Problem Specification: Axis-Aligned Rectangle Packing Optimization 1. Domain and Constraints - Bounding Box: A 2D plane defined by the square S = [0, 10000] x [0, 10000]. - Input Data: A set of N advertisers, where each advertiser i is defined by a tuple (xi, yi, Ai): (xi, yi): A target coordinate point such that 0 <= xi, yi <= 10000. Ai: A target area Ai > 0. - Decision Variables: For each advertiser i, define an axis-aligned rectangle Ri by its bounds [xi,min, xi,max] x [yi,min, yi,max]. 2. Geometric and Logical Constraints - Containment: For every i, (xi, yi) is in Ri. - Boundary Constraint: For every i, Ri is a subset of S (i.e., 0 <= xi,min < xi,max <= 10000 and 0 <= yi,min < yi,max <= 10000). - Non-overlapping Constraint: For all i != j, the interiors of Ri and Rj must be disjoint: int(Ri) intersection int(Rj) = empty set. 3. Objective Function Maximize the total advertiser satisfaction Z. While the exact scoring function is implicit, the goal is to minimize the deviation of the actual area ai = (xi,max - xi,min) * (yi,max - yi,min) from the target area Ai, subject to the non-overlapping constraint. Formally, maximize: sum of f(ai, Ai) for i=1 to N where f is a satisfaction function that penalizes |ai - Ai| and potentially penalizes ai = 0 or ai being significantly different from Ai. 4. Output Format Provide a list of rectangles, one for each advertiser i, represented as four coordinates: (xi,min, yi,min, xi,max, yi,max). Required Output Structure: FINAL_ANSWER: <List of N rectangles> JUSTIFICATION: <Brief description of the packing heuristic used>
